reasoning: San Diego FC is an expansion team entering Major League Soccer (MLS) in 2025. The current MLS playoff structure allows 9 out of 15 teams per conference (60%) to make the postseason, which provides a high base rate for any given team. However, expansion teams historically face a steep learning curve in their inaugural season. Looking at recent MLS expansion clubs since 2017, about half (Atlanta, LAFC, Nashville, Inter Miami, St. Louis) made the playoffs in year one, while others (Minnesota, Cincinnati, Austin, Charlotte) missed out. San Diego FC has strong financial backing and ownership connections (Right to Dream academy, FC Nordsjælland) and has already secured marquee signings like Hirving 'Chucky' Lozano, but building a cohesive roster from scratch under a first-time MLS head coach (Mikey Varas) carries significant variance. Taking an average of the generous 60% baseline playoff spot availability and the historically coin-flip nature of heavily-invested expansion sides adapting to MLS parity, combined with the difficulty of Western Conference competition, 42% reflects a balanced view of their inaugural playoff chances.
